message:
docs: narrow VCS_INTEGRATION.md git-invocation claims (beads_rust-3wzy)

Lines 3 and 10 claimed "br never runs version-control commands" and
"br does not run git". In reality, changelog, orphans, and stats read
git history via Command::new("git") when explicitly invoked. Reworded
to match the precise AGENTS.md/README phrasing: br never *automatically*
runs git; user-invoked reporting commands read (never write) git state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"beads_rust-3uxa","title":"Self-update command: br update with signature verification","description":"# Self-Update Command Implementation

## Scope
Implement `br update` command for in-place binary updates with cryptographic verification.

## Deliverables
- `br update` - Check for and install updates
- `br update --check` - Check only, don't install
- `br update --force` - Force reinstall current version
- `br update --version <VERSION>` - Install specific version
- Ed25519 signature verification of downloaded binaries
- SHA256 hash verification as fallback
- Streaming downloads for large files with progress
- Atomic update (download to temp, verify, replace)
- Rollback on failure (preserve original binary)
- Version comparison (semver-aware)

## Technical Approach
- Use self_update crate (rustls backend, no OpenSSL)
- GitHub Releases as update source
- .sig files for Ed25519 signatures
- .sha256 files for hash verification

## Acceptance Criteria
- `br update` downloads and installs newer version in-place
- `br update --check` exits 0 if up-to-date, 1 if update available
- Update fails safely if signature invalid (original binary preserved)
- Update works even if current binary is corrupted
- Progress shown during download
- Works on all platforms (Linux, macOS, Windows)

## Unit Tests (src/cli/commands/update.rs)
- test_version_comparison: verify semver comparison logic
- test_signature_verification: verify Ed25519 signature validation
- test_hash_verification: verify SHA256 hash checking
- test_release_parsing: verify GitHub Releases API response parsing
- test_rollback_on_failure: verify original binary restored on error
- test_platform_asset_selection: verify correct binary selected per platform

## E2E Tests (tests/e2e_update.rs)
- e2e_update_check_uptodate: when on latest, exit 0 and print "up to date"
- e2e_update_check_available: when older, exit 1 and print available version
- e2e_update_to_latest: verify successful update replaces binary
- e2e_update_force: verify --force reinstalls current version
- e2e_update_bad_signature: verify update aborted, original preserved
- e2e_update_network_failure: verify graceful error handling
- e2e_update_specific_version: verify --version flag works

## Logging Requirements
- [UPDATE] Checking for updates...
- [UPDATE] Current: v0.9.0, Latest: v1.0.0
- [DOWNLOAD] Downloading br-v1.0.0-linux-x86_64.tar.gz (27.4MB)
- [VERIFY] Signature: VALID (Ed25519)
- [VERIFY] SHA256: OK
- [UPDATE] Replacing binary...
- [SUCCESS] Updated to v1.0.0","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BronzeHawk","created_at":"2026-01-21T00:49:46.653237917Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T04:52:54.886780908Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T04:52:54.886190436Z","close_reason":"Implemented self-update with signature verification. Generated Ed25519 keypair and committed public key. Note: private key was not committed.","created_at":"2026-01-21T00:57:40Z"},{"id":62,"issue_id":"beads_rust-3uxa","author":"CopperMountain","text":"## Signature Verification Status Analysis

**Current State:** Signature verification code is commented out in upgrade.rs (lines 242-249, 260-269).

**Critical Finding:** Format mismatch between release pipeline and self_update crate:
- Release workflow uses minisign (.minisig format)
- self_update crate expects raw Ed25519 signatures (.sig files)

These formats are incompatible. The release_public_key.bin (32 bytes raw Ed25519) cannot verify minisign signatures.

**Options:**
1. Change release workflow to generate raw Ed25519 .sig files
2. Use only SHA256 checksum verification (already working)
3. Add minisign verification as separate post-download step

**Recommendation:** Keep signature verification disabled until format is aligned. Current upgrade implementation (--check, --force, --version, --dry-run, JSON/rich output) is complete and functional.","created_at":"2026-01-21T03:05:08Z"}]}

+{"id":"beads_rust-3wzy","title":"[LOW] VCS_INTEGRATION.md overstates 'br does not run git' guarantee","description":"VCS_INTEGRATION.md lines 3 and 10 claim:
  > br is VCS-agnostic and never runs version-control commands.
  > br does not run git — all VCS operations are explicit and manual.

These claims are too absolute. Reality:
- src/cli/commands/changelog.rs: 13 Command::new("git") invocations
- src/cli/commands/orphans.rs: 3 Command::new("git") invocations
- src/cli/commands/stats.rs: 4 Command::new("git") invocations

README.md already documents this correctly at lines 131-134:
  > git-reporting commands such as 'br changelog', 'br orphans', and
  > commit-activity 'br stats' inspect git history.

AGENTS.md is also precisely scoped (line 466):
  > 'br' is non-invasive—it NEVER runs git commands *automatically*.

The drift is only in VCS_INTEGRATION.md, which is authored in absolute terms.

Proposed fix: rewrite VCS_INTEGRATION.md lines 3-10 to match the README /
AGENTS.md phrasing — 'br never automatically runs version-control commands;
user-invoked reporting commands (changelog, orphans, stats) read git history.'

Verification:
  grep -rnE 'Command::new\("git"\)' src/cli/commands/

Prior audit trail: beads_rust-988p (closed) aligned README safety model with
actual sync/git behavior; VCS_INTEGRATION.md was not touched in that pass.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:53:00.009704049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:56:18.617170543Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T20:56:18.615503920Z","close_reason":"narrowed VCS_INTEGRATION.md claims to match actual code behavior","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"beads_rust-hee","title":"quick Command Implementation","description":"## Overview
Implement the `br quick` command for rapid issue lookup by ID prefix with minimal output. Designed for scripting and quick checks.

## CLI Interface
```
br quick <partial-id> [OPTIONS]

Arguments:
  <partial-id>              Partial issue ID to resolve

Options:
  --field <FIELD>           Output specific field only (id, title, status, priority, type)
  --json                    Output as JSON
```

## Technical Requirements

### Implementation
```rust
fn cmd_quick(partial: &str, field: Option<&str>) -> Result<()> {
    let storage = open_storage()?;
    let full_id = storage.resolve_partial_id(partial)?;
    let issue = storage.get_issue(&full_id)?
        .ok_or_else(|| BeadsError::IssueNotFound(partial.into()))?;
    
    match field {
        Some("id") => println!("{}", issue.id),
        Some("title") => println!("{}", issue.title),
        Some("status") => println!("{}", issue.status),
        Some("priority") => println!("{}", issue.priority),
        Some("type") => println!("{}", issue.issue_type),
        None => println!("{} {} {}", issue.id, issue.status, issue.title),
        Some(f) => return Err(BeadsError::UnknownField(f.into())),
    }
    Ok(())
}
```

### Use Cases
```bash
# Get full ID from prefix
br quick abc
# Output: bd-abc12

# Get just the title
br quick abc --field title
# Output: Fix authentication bug

# Use in scripts
STATUS=$(br quick abc --field status)
if [ "$STATUS" = "closed" ]; then
    echo "Already done"
fi
```

## Output Formats

### Default (single line)
```
bd-abc12 open Fix authentication bug
```

### --field (single value)
```
open
```

### --json
```json
{
  "id": "bd-abc12",
  "title": "Fix authentication bug",
  "status": "open",
  "priority": 1,
  "issue_type": "bug"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Resolve partial ID to full ID
- [ ] Output single line by default
- [ ] --field outputs just that field
- [ ] --json outputs full issue as JSON
- [ ] Error if ambiguous prefix
- [ ] Error if no match

## Unit Tests
- Exact ID match works
- Prefix match works
- Ambiguous prefix returns error
- No match returns error
- Each field flag works
- JSON output correct

## Dependencies
- ID Resolution & Prefix Matching
- SQLite Storage Layer Core

## Rationale
The quick command enables scripting and fast lookups. Its minimal output makes it ideal for shell pipelines and scripts that need issue data.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:19:27.461086167Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T07:50:01.941161218Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T07:50:01.941161218Z","close_reason":"Superseded by beads_rust-k0w (q quick capture command)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}

-{"id":"beads_rust-hkgq","title":"[MEDIUM] MCP create_issue swallows ID existence lookup failures","description":"Subsystem: src/mcp/.

Causal finding: MCP create_issue must guarantee generated IDs are based on a reliable view of existing issue IDs. At src/mcp/tools.rs:1281 and src/mcp/tools.rs:1291, storage.id_exists(...).unwrap_or(false) treats any storage/query error as 'ID does not exist'. The analogous CLI path in src/cli/commands/create.rs:388-398 records id_exists errors and returns them, so this MCP path has weaker failure semantics.

Counterfactual finding: if the assumption 'id_exists cannot fail while storage is open' is removed, MCP can continue ID generation after a failed lookup. In the parent-child path it may accept the first candidate after an error; in the hash-ID path it may accept a candidate without proving uniqueness. A later insert may fail with a less precise error, or a transient lookup error may be masked entirely.

Adversarial finding: a concurrent or malicious local caller that can induce SQLite query errors/lock pressure during MCP create_issue can cause MCP to proceed from stale/unknown ID-existence information. The write lock protects br callers, but this code still explicitly discards the storage error instead of surfacing it as an MCP ToolExecutionError.

Proposed fix: mirror the CLI generate_new_id behavior: capture any id_exists error during hash ID generation and return it after generation, treat lookup errors as 'exists' while retrying, and add a bounded retry loop for parent-child ID generation with explicit error propagation. Add MCP unit tests that inject an id_exists failure or use a poisoned/malformed storage path and assert create_issue fails before returning success.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:45:09.899695207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:45:09.899695207Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mcp","modes-of-reasoning","multi-perspective"]}

 {"id":"beads_rust-hn1o","title":"Conformance harness: read-only bd↔br parity","description":"Build conformance runs that compare bd and br on read-only commands using the same datasets.

## Scope
Run read-only commands with --json on identical dataset copies and verify parity.

### Commands to Test
**Core List/Query:**
- `list` (with all filter combinations: --status, --type, --priority, --label, --assignee)
- `show <id>` (single issue, with --json)
- `search <query>` (full-text search)
- `ready` (unblocked issues)
- `blocked` (blocked issues with reasons)

**Statistics/Reports:**
- `stats` / `status` (project statistics)
- `count` (issue counts with grouping)
- `stale` (stale issues by age threshold)
- `orphans` (orphaned issues referenced in commits)
- `changelog` (changelog generation)

**Dependencies/Structure:**
- `dep list <id>` (list dependencies)
- `dep tree <id>` (dependency tree)
- `graph` (full dependency graph)
- `epic status` (epic progress)

**Workspace/Config:**
- `info` (workspace info)
- `where` (workspace path)
- `version` (version info)
- `doctor` (diagnostic checks)
- `config list` (configuration)

### Normalization Rules
- Sort arrays by stable key (id for issues, created_at for comments)
- Mask volatile timestamps (created_at, updated_at) with [TIMESTAMP]
- Normalize paths (/ vs \ on Windows)
- Normalize line endings (CRLF -> LF)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All listed commands produce identical JSON output for br and bd on same dataset
- [ ] conformance_runs.jsonl emitted with timing + SHA-256 hashes per run
- [ ] JUnit XML report for CI integration
- [ ] Clear field-level diffs on failure with explanation
- [ ] Tests skip gracefully when bd binary unavailable

## Test Coverage (tests/conformance.rs)
- conformance_list_* (14+ tests for list command variants)
- conformance_ready_* (8+ tests for ready command)
- conformance_blocked_* (6+ tests for blocked command)
- conformance_show_* (tests for show command)
- conformance_search_* (tests for search command)
- conformance_stats_* (tests for stats/count commands)
- conformance_dep_* (tests for dependency commands)
- conformance_graph_* (tests for graph commands)

## Logging Requirements
- [CONFORMANCE] Running: br list --json
- [CONFORMANCE] Running: bd list --json
- [COMPARE] br stdout SHA256: abc123...
- [COMPARE] bd stdout SHA256: abc123...
- [PASS/FAIL] list --json: parity OK / diffs at .issues[0].updated_at

## Notes
- Working Go bd version: v0.47.1 (v0.47.2 has persistence bug)
- Use BD_CONFORMANCE_PATH env var or PATH override for bd binary","notes":"CONFORMANCE TESTING COMPLETE (2026-01-21):

Maintenance:
  rename-prefix      Rename the issue prefix for all issues in the database

  resolve-conflicts  Resolve git merge conflicts in JSONL files

Integrations & Advanced:
Working With Issues:
  close              Close one or more issues
  comments           View or manage comments on an issue
  create             Create a new issue (or multiple issues from markdown file)
  create-form        Create a new issue using an interactive form
  delete             Delete one or more issues and clean up references
  edit               Edit an issue field in $EDITOR
  gate               Manage async coordination gates
  label              Manage issue labels
  list               List issues
  merge-slot         Manage merge-slot gates for serialized conflict resolution
  move               Move an issue to a different rig with dependency remapping
  q                  Quick capture: create issue and output only ID
  refile             Move an issue to a different rig
  reopen             Reopen one or more closed issues
  search             Search issues by text query
  set-state          Set operational state (creates event + updates label)
  show               Show issue details
  state              Query the current value of a state dimension
  update             Update one or more issues

Views & Reports:
  activity           Show real-time molecule state feed
  count              Count issues matching filters
  lint               Check issues for missing template sections
  stale              Show stale issues (not updated recently)
  status             Show issue database overview and statistics

Dependencies & Structure:
  dep                Manage dependencies
  duplicate          Mark an issue as a duplicate of another
  duplicates         Find and optionally merge duplicate issues
  epic               Epic management commands
  graph              Display issue dependency graph
  supersede          Mark an issue as superseded by a newer one
  swarm              Swarm management for structured epics

Sync & Data:
  daemon             Manage background sync daemon
  export             Export issues to JSONL or Obsidian format
  import             Import issues from JSONL format
  merge              Git merge driver for beads JSONL files
  restore            Restore full history of a compacted issue from git
  sync               Synchronize issues with git remote

Setup & Configuration:
  config             Manage configuration settings
  hooks              Manage git hooks for bd auto-sync
  human              Show essential commands for human users
  info               Show database and daemon information
  init               Initialize bd in the current directory
  onboard            Display minimal snippet for AGENTS.md
  prime              Output AI-optimized workflow context

  preflight          Show PR readiness checklist
  upgrade            Check and manage bd version upgrades
  worktree           Manage git worktrees for parallel development

Integrations & Advanced:
  admin              Administrative commands for database maintenance
  jira               Jira integration commands
  linear             Linear integration commands
  repo               Manage multiple repository configuration

Additional Commands:
  agent              Manage agent bead state
  audit              Record and label agent interactions (append-only JSONL)
  blocked            Show blocked issues
  completion         Generate the autocompletion script for the specified shell
  cook               Compile a formula into a proto (ephemeral by default)
  defer              Defer one or more issues for later
  formula            Manage workflow formulas
  help               Help about any command
  mail               Delegate to mail provider (e.g., gt mail)
  mol                Molecule commands (work templates)
  orphans            Identify orphaned issues (referenced in commits but still open)
  ready              Show ready work (no blockers, open or in_progress)
  ship               Publish a capability for cross-project dependencies
  slot               Manage agent bead slots
  undefer            Undefer one or more issues (restore to open)
  version            Print version information

      --actor string            Actor name for audit trail (default: $BD_ACTOR, git user.name, $USER)

Testing bd v0.46.0 binary (/data/tmp/bd-v0.46.0):
- bd v0.46.0 can successfully init and create/list issues in isolation (verified manually)
- However, conformance tests still fail: 3 passed, 11 failed

Root cause identified:
1. Tests use init_both() which creates separate br/bd workspaces
2. Tests then use run_br_in_bd_env() to create issues (because 'bd create is flaky' per code comment)
3. bd list returns empty because bd can't read issues created by br

This is a schema incompatibility issue, not just a bd binary bug. As noted in earlier comments, bd expects 'crystallizes' column that br doesn't produce.

Recommendation: Conformance testing needs one of:
- Schema alignment work to make br/bd databases compatible
- A different testing strategy that doesn't require cross-tool database access
- Using JSONL export/import for data sharing between br and bd workspaces","created_at":"2026-01-21T00:36:11Z"},{"id":140,"issue_id":"beads_rust-hn1o","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Schema comparison (OrangeGrove):

BD v0.46.0 has these extra columns not in BR:
- mol_type, event_kind, actor, target, payload (molecule/event fields)
- hook_bead, role_bead, agent_state, last_activity, role_type, rig (agent/rig fields)

BR has columns not in BD v0.46.0:
- owner, source_system

This schema divergence is BY DESIGN - br implements 'classic beads' while bd has evolved toward GasTown features (molecules, rigs, agents).

Conformance testing options:
1. JSONL-based: Both tools export to JSONL, compare at that layer
2. Schema bridge: Create migration to add missing columns
3. Read-only parity: Focus on query output normalization, not cross-DB access

JSONL-based approach seems most aligned with the hybrid SQLite+JSONL architecture.","created_at":"2026-01-21T00:36:56Z"},{"id":141,"issue_id":"beads_rust-hn1o","author":"OrangeGrove (Claude)","text":"## Final Conformance Test Results (bd v0.46.0)

Full test suite run: BD_BINARY=/data/tmp/bd-v0.46.0 cargo test --test conformance

**Results: 319 passed, 14 failed, 11 ignored**

### Failing Tests (14):
1. conformance_list_* tests (8 tests) - bd returns empty results when reading br-created issues due to schema mismatch
2. conformance_graph_compact_flag - bd v0.46.0 lacks --compact flag  
3. conformance_sync_base_snapshot_created_after_sync - git-related failure
4. Various other sync/export tests affected by schema incompatibility

### Root Cause:
The failures are NOT due to bd binary bugs but rather schema evolution divergence:
- bd evolved toward GasTown with new columns (mol_type, event_kind, actor, target, payload, hook_bead, role_bead, agent_state, last_activity, role_type, rig)
- br implements classic beads with different columns (owner, source_system)

### What Works:
- JSONL-based conformance: conformance_sync_flush_only PASSES - comparing JSONL exports works
- Direct bd operations in isolation work fine with v0.46.0
- 319 tests pass including all ready/blocked logic tests

### Recommendation:
Focus conformance testing on JSONL interchange format rather than cross-database access. This is the realistic interop scenario - teams will sync via JSONL, not share SQLite files.

 {"id":"beads_rust-w1i","title":"lint Command Implementation","description":"# lint Command (template validation)

## Purpose
Read-only validation of required template headings in issue descriptions. This is classic bd behavior (substring match, case-insensitive), not a strict Markdown parser.

## CLI
```
br lint [issue-id...] [--type <type>] [--status <status>]
```
Defaults:
- If no IDs: lint open issues only.
- Daemon mode excluded; direct storage only.

## Required Sections (classic)
- **bug**: "Steps to Reproduce" + "Acceptance Criteria"
- **task/feature**: "Acceptance Criteria"
- **epic**: "Success Criteria"
- Other types: no requirements

Matching rules:
- Case-insensitive substring search.
- Markdown prefixes (`#`, `##`) stripped before matching.

## JSON Output
```json
{
  "total": 3,
  "issues": 2,
  "results": [
    { "id": "bd-abc", "title": "Fix", "type": "bug", "missing": ["## Steps to Reproduce"], "warnings": 1 }
  ]
}
```
Notes:
- `results` includes only issues with warnings.
- JSON mode exits 0 even with warnings.
- Human mode exits 1 if warnings exist.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Required headings matched case-insensitively.
- JSON and human outputs match bd.

## Tests
- Issue descriptions with and without required headings.
- Exit code differences between JSON and human modes.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:17:50.930149167Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T03:36:25.244001818Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T03:36:25.244001818Z","close_reason":"Lint command fully implemented: template section validation for bug/task/feature/epic types with case-insensitive matching, JSON output support, exit codes per spec","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
